headlines.title: Police officer, 19, dies from crash injuries
headlines.description: Jess Turnbull "had so much to look forward to in her career and her life", the chief constable says.

headlines.title: Olivia Rodrigo on heartbreak and choosing her wedding song
headlines.description: The pop star discusses the difficulty of writing happy songs, and her Glastonbury anxiety attack.

headlines.title: Facing a seismic by-election, the people of Makerfield tell us what matters to them
headlines.description: In conversations with residents, business owners and campaigners in Makerfield, the BBC found a mixed mood.

headlines.title: Ryanair investigated over charging parents to sit with children
headlines.description: The UK's competition regulator is investigating the airline over charges it imposes on parents to sit next to their child.

headlines.title: Jeffrey Donaldson takes the stand in sex abuse trial
headlines.description: Sir Jeffrey Donaldson, the former DUP leader, is on trial for 18 sex abuse charges, including one count of rape, all of which he denies.

headlines.title: Nearly 3,000 patients a day in England face corridor care in NHS
headlines.description: New data reveals sheer scale of patients in England being treated in unsafe and undignified make-shift areas.

headlines.title: Dani Dyer returns to Strictly a year after pulling out with broken ankle
headlines.description: "Hopefully this time around I can actually make it to week one," the TV and social media star says.

headlines.title: 'Mum was killing me': England's Rice on World Cup heat - and his sunburn
headlines.description: The Arsenal midfielder admits he was a touch pink in pre-tournament photos as players try to adjust to scorching conditions.

headlines.title: Bellingham at number 10? Will Saka start? It's decision time for Tuchel
headlines.description: England's World Cup preparations are complete, so who will now make it into Thomas Tuchel's team to face Croatia?

headlines.title: Find your local World Cup hero with our postcode lookup
headlines.description: Which World Cup stars have roots near you? Find out with our interactive lookup tool

headlines.title: 'Absolutely buzzing': Scotland fans arrive on US soil
headlines.description: Thousands of Tartan Army members are travelling to Boston ahead of the country's first match at the weekend.

headlines.title: A sneak peek inside Mexico’s iconic Azteca Stadium before World Cup kickoff
headlines.description: The BBC's Will Grant got access inside the renovated venue ahead of the first game of the tournament.

headlines.title: 'A van was set alight and pushed towards my home'
headlines.description: People tell the BBC they are afraid to stay in their homes, as a nurse is confronted by masked men on her way to work during a second night of disorder in Belfast.

headlines.title: An Everest guide's miraculous survival raises questions for tourism industry
headlines.description: Why was a cook leading clients up the world's highest peak? And why was he left to rescue himself?

headlines.title: What we know about US sea drone used in helicopter crew rescue mission
headlines.description: BBC Verify examines what we know about the drone boat and how the mission took place.

headlines.title: Pope Leo visits Canary Islands to highlight perilous journeys of migrants
headlines.description: During his trip, the Pope will appeal for a humane approach and respectful welcome for migrants seeking a better life.

headlines.title: The furious dispute over what caused Air India flight 171 to crash
headlines.description: The final conclusions of the investigation have yet to be published, although more could become apparent in the coming days.

headlines.title: Serena Williams' run at Queen's over as partner Victoria Mboko withdraws
headlines.description: Serena Williams' doubles campaign at Queen's is curtailed as her playing partner Victoria Mboko withdraws with a left knee injury.

headlines.title: Vance says Israeli PM Netanyahu 'has got some things wrong'
headlines.description: The US vice-president says the Israeli leader "aggressively asserts" his country's interests, but they do not always align with those of the US.

headlines.title: Mum's plea to reopen case into daughter's shooting in US
headlines.description: The mother of Lucy Harrison, who was shot by her father at his Texas home, demands further inquiries.

headlines.title: 'Hidden house' farmer slams contempt conviction
headlines.description: Helen Lowe was found in contempt of court for failing to remove a house she built inside a barn.

headlines.title: Asylum seeker who raped woman, 18, jailed
headlines.description: Sheraz Malik, 28, was found guilty of two counts of rape by a jury in January.

headlines.title: Thai court sentences two men to death over Bangkok shrine bombing
headlines.description: Twenty people died and 120 others were injured in the explosion at the Erawan shrine 11 years ago.

headlines.title: Taylor Swift and Timothee Chalamet among fans for historic Knicks win
headlines.description: A star-studded crowd saw the New York Knicks score a winning basket against the San Antonio Spurs in the last seconds of the game.

headlines.title: BBC News app
headlines.description: Top stories, breaking news, live reporting, and follow news topics that match your interests

headlines.title: The Secret Jungle Hospital
headlines.description: Saving lives in a hidden hospital, rebels fighting against Myanmar's military.

headlines.title: The Democrats fight back Trump-style in Maine… but have they messed up?
headlines.description: New scandals and allegations emerge against Democrat Graham Platner in Maine

headlines.title: Kevin Bridges asks if football still has beauty at its heart
headlines.description: Kevin Bridges embarks on a journey to understand whether the beautiful game still exists.

headlines.title: Messi, Maradona or Pele? Ranking the top 10 World Cup legends
headlines.description: Who are the biggest World Cup legends of all time? From Pele to Lionel Messi, we attempt to name the top 10.

headlines.title: Wolves sack Edwards after seven months
headlines.description: Wolves sack head coach Rob Edwards just seven months after appointing him.

headlines.title: O'Neill confirmed as Celtic's permanent manager
headlines.description: Martin O'Neill is confirmed as Celtic's permanent manager after two interim spells last season led to a league and cup double.

headlines.title: Spurs the dumbest basketball team in history of civilisation - Barkley
headlines.description: NBA legend Charles Barkley brands the San Antonio Spurs "the dumbest basketball team in the history of civilisation" after the New York Knicks move 3-1 ahead of the San Antonio Spurs.

headlines.title: Williams' Queen's run over as injured Mboko withdraws
headlines.description: Serena Williams' doubles campaign at Queen's is curtailed as her playing partner Victoria Mboko withdraws with a left knee injury.

headlines.title: Germany players pay for fans to travel to game
headlines.description: Germany's players will pay for 600 of the country's fans to travel by bus to their final World Cup group game in New Jersey amid criticism of increased transport costs at the tournament.
